programmes.show_description: Alf and Mrs Hollingbery are off to Australia to meet her rich brother and to get married. Alf is not sure which he is more frightened of - flying or getting married. S4 E5
With BA Robertson and Mel Smith. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy show from Thames TV, all in the best possible taste!
Simon gets involved when Mrs Yarmouth is convinced to hand over church funds by husband-and-wife confidence tricksters. S2 E14: The Bunco Artists
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Mata Hari became an icon of betrayal, accused of seducing secrets for espionage. But were the charges real, or was she a scapegoat in World War I hysteria? Narrated by Robert Powell.
Susan learns about the One O'Clock Gun in Edinburgh and the hiding place of Scotland's crown jewels at Edinburgh Castle before getting to grips with golf at St Andrews. S1 E1: Edinburgh
...Were You? On the 1st of September, 2004, terrorists took 1,100 people, including 777 children, hostage in the Beslan School in Russia - the ensuing massacre saw over 300 people killed.
Johnny Depp has become one of the most recognisable faces in Hollywood. We take a look at his career.
There would have been no London without the River Thames, and it grew firstly as a port and trading route; we go behind the scenes at Westminster, Chelsea, Greenwich and the Tower of London.
The Grumman TBF Avenger is an American WWII torpedo bomber developed initially for the United States Navy and Marine Corps and used by several air and naval aviation services around the world. S3 E6
The landing at Anzac Cove in April 1915 was part of the amphibious invasion of the Gallipoli Peninsula by the forces of the British Empire and began the land phase of the First World War.
Survivors recount the horrors behind surviving a merciless place and hear the shock of Allied soldiers as they discover the evil secret of Germany's concentration camps. Ep11: Fall of Berlin
The USA has been at the forefront of fighting acts of terror across the world. We look at the development of the Delta force and the use of Special Units in Iran, Grenada, Panama and the Gulf War.
Examining the history of war tactics. Special operations are the cutting edge of warfare. Undertaken by elite personnel, they can cover everything from intelligence gathering to raiding. S2 E5
The RAF had aircraft that could escort bombers to targets deep inside Germany and back by the end of World War One, and the Hawker Hunter and F-86 Sabre took the RAF supersonic. Ep3: A Vicious Sting
The story of Ewen Montagu and Operation Mincemeat; the Nazis were fooled into thinking that the initial landings would be through Greece rather than Sicily & Italy. Ep7: The Man Who Hoodwinked Hitler
As the future of Europe hung in the balance, a web of lies tied up German army divisions across Europe with the Allies concealing D-Day plans. S1 E12: D-Day Deceptions
The Free French Forces led by de Gaulle refused to accept the armistice their country had signed with Germany and crossed the Channel to join the fight in Britain. Ep12: The Free French Forces
There is much speculation as to why Jack is taking so long in Scarborough for the divorce from Dolly. Matt finds out Sir Horatio is dealing with the Germans. S3 E4: A Ticket To Care For The Wounded
Arthur is forced to broadcast his first-ever commercial on pirate radio when a rival car dealer tries to put him out of business with a nightmare test drive.
An election threatens Truro's status quo. As his past actions confront him, Ross senses a rival for Demelza's affection. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E8
Alex thinks she's close to death and has to keep her brain alive by solving a raid at a Post Office; Gene believes the culprit is Chas Cale, a blagger he once crossed. S1 E6
Alf, Mrs Hollingbery and Arthur have flown to Australia to meet Mrs Hollingbery's rich brother Ricky. He manages to convince the three poms to go sightseeing for the day. S4 E6
With Billy Connolly, Tom O'Connor and The Pretenders. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy show from Thames TV, all in the best possible taste!
Can an art gallery really be burglar or Saint-proof? Simon believes not and wagers 5,000 pounds to state his case; is an ulterior motive at play? S2 E15: The Benevolent Burglary

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
The SS began as Hitler's bodyguard, then grew into a vast force. After defeat, ODESSA aided escapes, through figures like Eichmann and Barbie. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Susan goes on a trip to Stirling - Scotland's historical heartland - and learns what it's like to hold William Wallace's sword at the iconic Wallace Monument. S1 E2: Stirling
There are some news events that change the world. We look at the war in Afghanistan and the attempts to defeat the Taliban.
A look into the lives of the rich and famous; Cameron Diaz burst onto the big screen in 'The Mask' in 1994 and has been in the spotlight ever since.
We delve into London's past exploring the castles and fortresses that over the centuries have safe-guarded the Capital including Windsor Castle - the world's largest inhabited castle
The contribution of two million Asians who participated in the First World War is entirely forgotten today - this series explores how the conflict shaped the continent. E1 - India: The Forgotten Army
The Battle of the Somme began in July 1916; more than three million men fought in the battle and one million men were wounded or killed, making it one of the deadliest battles in human history.
The war in the Pacific nears its violent end as a drastic tactic triggers the nuclear age - brave men share their thoughts behind the war's most tragic events. Ep12: Final Victory
We explore America's fight against terrorism, looking at 21st Century warfare with the special forces' actions during the Battle of the Black Sea, 9/11, and the hunt for Osama Bin Laden.
James Holland and Mike Simpson examine the preparations for the invasion across southern England before crossing the Channel to Normandy and challenging many D-Day myths. S1 E1: D-Day
The RAF had bombers that could fly to Berlin and back by the end of the First World War, but British civilians found themselves under direct attack by Zeppelin airships. Ep4: Strike Hard, Strike Sure
The story of the liberation of Belsen concentration camp by the British Army, what they found, and how they let the world know how they treated the survivors. Ep8: The Man Who Liberated Belsen
After Nazi Germany's invasion and capture of France, brave French citizens take it upon themselves to wage a guerrilla campaign against their occupiers. S1 E13: The French Resistance
We take a look at the Australian and New Zealand Army Corps (Anzacs) who first saw active service in Libya with the British when they forced the Italian army to surrender. Ep13: The Anzacs
Sir Horatio Manners draws up a contract with Jack as Managing Director of his company and Jack insists on an extra clause to protect the jobs of his Union men. S3 E5: Travel Light, Travel Far
Arthur is being targeted by violent criminal Charlie Knowles, who is doing time for armed robbery and wants vengeance because Arthur sold him the getaway car.
The Poldark family finds itself challenged on all fronts and threatened as never before. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E9
Drama series following the exploits of Life on Mars DCI Gene Hunt. When thousands of pounds of charity money is stolen, Gene resorts to his 'fists first, questions later' method. S1 E7
